code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 design identification only

reliability.note: Confidence rating provides honest uncertainty labeling; one gap: no behavior defined for when paper methods are deliberately obfuscated

agent_specific.note: Primary/secondary/hybrid distinction is a strong differentiator; self-label correction feature is valuable and rare

inputs.label: Classify a paper self-described as 'real-world evidence' from methods

assertions.text: Design identified from actual methods structure, not author self-description
Primary design label assigned with justification
Self-label corrected if imprecise with explanation
No fabricated study features to support classification

inputs.label: Hybrid paper: GEO screening + TCGA validation + mouse experiments

assertions.text: Hybrid status identified and not forced into single oversimplified label
Primary and secondary design layers separated
Nearest confusing alternative designs explained in Section E
Evidence family position placed for downstream appraisal use
What design can/cannot support stated in Section H

inputs.label: Only title and journal available — design cannot be determined

assertions.text: Low classification confidence assigned with specific reason
No design invented from title alone
User asked to provide abstract or methods for higher confidence classification
Any inferences from title labeled as speculative
Most likely classification provided with major ambiguity explicitly stated

key_strengths: Self-label correction feature explicitly corrects misleading author terminology — a rare and valuable capability
Primary/secondary/hybrid tripartite classification prevents false single-label oversimplification for complex modern papers
Classification confidence rating ensures honest uncertainty disclosure when methods are incomplete
Twelve hard rules precisely prevent data-type/design conflation and label inflation

recommendations.title: No behavior defined for deliberately obfuscated methods sections
recommendations.problem: The skill handles incomplete methods by lowering confidence, but does not address the scenario where methods appear to be strategically vague or deliberately non-reproducible.
recommendations.root_cause: Coverage rules focus on availability not intentionality of incompleteness.
recommendations.fix: Add a methods-quality note: if multiple design-critical details appear systematically omitted (not just one or two), flag this as 'Methods Opacity' and recommend the user contact authors or seek supplementary materials before high-confidence classification.

recommendations.title: Edge-case handling reference could be more explicitly prominent
recommendations.problem: The edge-case-handling.md reference is listed but its activation criteria are not clearly specified in SKILL.md for agents that may miss triggering it for mislabeled papers.
recommendations.root_cause: Reference module integration section does not specify which input conditions trigger the edge-case-handling reference.
recommendations.fix: Add a trigger note: 'Use references/edge-case-handling.md whenever the paper's self-label appears to conflict with structural signals extracted in Step 2, or when the paper appears to be a non-standard design type'.
